description: Pings from the standby switch to VRRP IPs are not successful, resulting in traffic loss from the access layer to the edge.

messages.content: For 9300 AOS-CX 10.17.1010, what caveat is documented for L3 Addressing?
Feature Caveat: L3 Addressing - Pings from the standby switch to VRRP IPs are not successful, resulting in traffic loss from the access layer to the edge.

description: When no dhcp-relay l2vpn-clients is configured and the client MAC is not learned then the first L2VPN DHCP client packet is dropped.

messages.content: For 9300 AOS-CX 10.17.1010, what caveat is documented for DHCP Relay?
Feature Caveat: DHCP Relay - When no dhcp-relay l2vpn-clients is configured and the client MAC is not learned then the first L2VPN DHCP client packet is dropped.

description: When configuring the same BGP neighbor under both IPv4 and IPv6 address families on a router, if a maximum prefix limit is set on one address family (like IPv4), exceeding that limit can potentially impact the BGP session for the other address family (IPv6) as well.

messages.content: For 9300 AOS-CX 10.17.1010, what caveat is documented for BGP?

Feature Caveat: BGP - When configuring the same BGP neighbor under both IPv4 and IPv6 address families on a router, if a maximum prefix limit is set on one address family (like IPv4), exceeding that limit can potentially impact the BGP session for the other address family (IPv6) as well.

description: If there is no user configuration for DNS servers and HPE Aruba Networking Central is configured, the switch defaults to using Google's DNS server at 8.8.8.8. HPE Aruba Networking Central is enabled by default across all switch platforms.

messages.content: For 9300 AOS-CX 10.17.1010, what caveat is documented for Activate?

Feature Caveat: Activate - If there is no user configuration for DNS servers and HPE Aruba Networking Central is configured, the switch defaults to using Google's DNS server at 8.8.8.8. HPE Aruba Networking Central is enabled by default across all switch platforms.

description: When a switch can connect to HPE Aruba Networking Central but is not registered in the inventory or lacks a proper license, it will repeatedly disconnect and reconnect. This cycle will continue until the switch is properly registered or a license is obtained. To avoid this unnecessary reconnection cycle, it is best practice to disable HPE Aruba Networking Central until the switch is registered or a license is obtained.

messages.content: For 9300 AOS-CX 10.17.1010, what caveat is documented for Central?

Feature Caveat: Central - When a switch can connect to HPE Aruba Networking Central but is not registered in the inventory or lacks a proper license, it will repeatedly disconnect and reconnect. This cycle will continue until the switch is properly registered or a license is obtained. To avoid this unnecessary reconnection cycle, it is best practice to disable HPE Aruba Networking Central until the switch is registered or a license is obtained.

description: When a switch uses a certificate with a legacy certificate name that is not supported in 10.12 because it contains disallowed characters, the information will migrate properly in the upgrade, but that certificate can no longer be edited. For new certificate names, only alphanumeric characters, dots, dashes, and underscores are allowed.

messages.content: For 9300 AOS-CX 10.17.1010, what caveat is documented for Certificates?

Feature Caveat: Certificates - When a switch uses a certificate with a legacy certificate name that is not supported in 10.12 because it contains disallowed characters, the information will migrate properly in the upgrade, but that certificate can no longer be edited. For new certificate names, only alphanumeric characters, dots, dashes, and underscores are allowed.

description: Policies containing both MAC and IPv6 classes are not allowed.

messages.content: For 9300 AOS-CX 10.17.1010, what caveat is documented for Classifiers?
Feature Caveat: Classifiers - Policies containing both MAC and IPv6 classes are not allowed.

description: For secure classifier policy modifications, HPE Aruba Networking strongly encourages a three-step process: bring down the port, make the modifications, and then bring the port back up.

messages.content: For 9300 AOS-CX 10.17.1010, what limitation is mentioned for Classifiers?

Feature Caveat: Classifiers - For secure classifier policy modifications, HPE Aruba Networking strongly encourages a three-step process: bring down the port, make the modifications, and then bring the port back up.

description: No other checkpoint besides "startup-configuration" gets migrated during the upgrade process.

messages.content: For 9300 AOS-CX 10.17.1010, what caveat is documented for CMF?
Feature Caveat: CMF - No other checkpoint besides "startup-configuration" gets migrated during the upgrade process.

description: When downloading a hot-patch file using the switch WebUI, log messages may incorrectly state that the file is added to the database with a missing status. This is a temporary state, and will correctly change to Not applied once the download is completed.

messages.content: For 9300 AOS-CX 10.17.1010, what caveat is documented for Hot Patch?

Feature Caveat: Hot Patch - When downloading a hot-patch file using the switch WebUI, log messages may incorrectly state that the file is added to the database with a missing status. This is a temporary state, and will correctly change to Not applied once the download is completed.

feature: IGMP/PIM on 6-in-6, Loopback and GRE interfaces
description: IGMP cannot be enabled on either Loopback or GRE interfaces. IGMP and PIM is not supported on a 6-in-6 Tunnel.

messages.content: For 9300 AOS-CX 10.17.1010, what caveat is documented for IGMP/PIM on 6-in-6, Loopback and GRE interfaces?
Feature Caveat: IGMP/PIM on 6-in-6, Loopback and GRE interfaces - IGMP cannot be enabled on either Loopback or GRE interfaces. IGMP and PIM is not supported on a 6-in-6 Tunnel.

description: Do not use reserved ports or ports used by other applications/services for different services. When two services use the same port, unexpected behaviors may occur. Best practice is to assign a unique port for each service across the system.

messages.content: For 9300 AOS-CX 10.17.1010, what caveat is documented for IP-SLA?

Feature Caveat: IP-SLA - Do not use reserved ports or ports used by other applications/services for different services. When two services use the same port, unexpected behaviors may occur. Best practice is to assign a unique port for each service across the system.

description: In an environment with devices running AOS-Switch, do not enable UDLD on the same link. The UDLD session can toggle between up and down continuously when both MACsec and UDLD is enabled on the same link.

messages.content: For 9300 AOS-CX 10.17.1010, what caveat is documented for MACsec and UDLD?

Feature Caveat: MACsec and UDLD - In an environment with devices running AOS-Switch, do not enable UDLD on the same link. The UDLD session can toggle between up and down continuously when both MACsec and UDLD is enabled on the same link.

description: (for 9300S Switch Series only) WAN MACsec with a custom ether type will not be established between CX devices when there are PVOS switches in the bypass.

messages.content: For 9300 AOS-CX 10.17.1010, what caveat is documented for MACsec?
Feature Caveat: MACsec - (for 9300S Switch Series only) WAN MACsec with a custom ether type will not be established between CX devices when there are PVOS switches in the bypass.

description: In an environment with Cisco devices, when the GCM-AES-XPN-128 or GCM-AES-XPN-256 cipher suite is used for establishing the MACsec channel, the MKA policy on the Cisco device must be configured with ssci-based-on-sci.

messages.content: For 9300 AOS-CX 10.17.1010, what limitation is mentioned for MACsec?

Feature Caveat: MACsec - In an environment with Cisco devices, when the GCM-AES-XPN-128 or GCM-AES-XPN-256 cipher suite is used for establishing the MACsec channel, the MKA policy on the Cisco device must be configured with ssci-based-on-sci.

description: (for 9300S Switch Series only) In an environment with a Cisco device, the Cisco device must be designated as the key server. Designating the AOS-CX as the key server results in complete traffic loss.

messages.content: For 9300 AOS-CX 10.17.1010, what additional caveat is documented for MACsec?

Feature Caveat: MACsec - (for 9300S Switch Series only) In an environment with a Cisco device, the Cisco device must be designated as the key server. Designating the AOS-CX as the key server results in complete traffic loss.

description: MACsec uses a software-based implementation to track start and stop times for secure channels and secure associations. As the implementation is software-based, the stop times for MACsec secure channel and secure associations are only updated when they are deleted and therefore never updated in the output of the show macsec status detailed command.

messages.content: For 9300 AOS-CX 10.17.1010, what additional caveat is documented for MACsec?

Feature Caveat: MACsec - MACsec uses a software-based implementation to track start and stop times for secure channels and secure associations. As the implementation is software-based, the stop times for MACsec secure channel and secure associations are only updated when they are deleted and therefore never updated in the output of the show macsec status detailed command.

description: (for 9300S Switch Series only) In an environment with Cisco and FlexFabric or H3C devices, do not update confidentiality-offset on the live channel. There can be complete traffic loss for an extended period on the MACsec channel when confidentiality-offset is updated on both ends.

messages.content: For 9300 AOS-CX 10.17.1010, what additional caveat is documented for MACsec?

Feature Caveat: MACsec - (for 9300S Switch Series only) In an environment with Cisco and FlexFabric or H3C devices, do not update confidentiality-offset on the live channel. There can be complete traffic loss for an extended period on the MACsec channel when confidentiality-offset is updated on both ends.

description: (for 9300S Switch Series only) In an environment with Juniper, configure no include-sci-tag under MACsec policy for data traffic forwarding.

messages.content: For 9300 AOS-CX 10.17.1010, what additional caveat is documented for MACsec?
Feature Caveat: MACsec - (for 9300S Switch Series only) In an environment with Juniper, configure no include-sci-tag under MACsec policy for data traffic forwarding.

description: Passing multicast traffic over GRE, 6in4 or 6in6 tunnels is not supported.

messages.content: For 9300 AOS-CX 10.17.1010, what caveat is documented for Multicast?
Feature Caveat: Multicast - Passing multicast traffic over GRE, 6in4 or 6in6 tunnels is not supported.

description: Pim Active-Active is not supported on overlay VXLAN SVIs.

messages.content: For 9300 AOS-CX 10.17.1010, what caveat is documented for PIM-SM?
Feature Caveat: PIM-SM - Pim Active-Active is not supported on overlay VXLAN SVIs.

description: REST supports the 'admin' and 'operator' roles but does not work with TACACS+ command authorization.

messages.content: For 9300 AOS-CX 10.17.1010, what caveat is documented for REST?
Feature Caveat: REST - REST supports the 'admin' and 'operator' roles but does not work with TACACS+ command authorization.

description: When SNMP is enabled via the switch CLI, it can take between 1-2 minutes for the SNMP daemon to be ready to respond to requests. If a local or external SNMP MIB walk is performed in the interval between when SNMP is first enabled and the SNMP daemon is ready, the MIB walk action will return an error.

messages.content: For 9300 AOS-CX 10.17.1010, what caveat is documented for SNMP?

Feature Caveat: SNMP - When SNMP is enabled via the switch CLI, it can take between 1-2 minutes for the SNMP daemon to be ready to respond to requests. If a local or external SNMP MIB walk is performed in the interval between when SNMP is first enabled and the SNMP daemon is ready, the MIB walk action will return an error.

description: BFD is not supported on a sub-interface. A sub-interface as underlay for EVPN-VXLAN is not supported

messages.content: For 9300 AOS-CX 10.17.1010, what caveat is documented for Sub-interface?
Feature Caveat: Sub-interface - BFD is not supported on a sub-interface. A sub-interface as underlay for EVPN-VXLAN is not supported.

description: Issuing the traceroute command with the ip-option loosesourceroute parameter fails in an overlay EVPN-VXLAN deployment.

messages.content: For 9300 AOS-CX 10.17.1010, what caveat is documented for Traceroute?
Feature Caveat: Traceroute - Issuing the traceroute command with the ip-option loosesourceroute parameter fails in an overlay EVPN-VXLAN deployment.

description: Traceroute v4/v6 over VXLAN fails to find intermediate next-hop IP information from a source VTEP in Virtual Active Gateway environment (the SVI is the same as theActive Gateway IP).

messages.content: For 9300 AOS-CX 10.17.1010, what limitation is mentioned for Traceroute?

Feature Caveat: Traceroute - Traceroute v4/v6 over VXLAN fails to find intermediate next-hop IP information from a source VTEP in Virtual Active Gateway environment (the SVI is the same as theActive Gateway IP).

description: When configuring GRE, 6in4 and 6in6 tunnels with the underlay as a static route, the next-hop IP should be an SVI or ROP IP and not configured as the Active-Gateway.

messages.content: For 9300 AOS-CX 10.17.1010, what caveat is documented for Tunnels?
Feature Caveat: Tunnels - When configuring GRE, 6in4 and 6in6 tunnels with the underlay as a static route, the next-hop IP should be an SVI or ROP IP and not configured as the Active-Gateway.

description: VXLAN must be configured prior to configuring VSX.

messages.content: For 9300 AOS-CX 10.17.1010, what caveat is documented for VXLAN?
Feature Caveat: VXLAN - VXLAN must be configured prior to configuring VSX.

description: Upgrading 9300/9300S devices from a build earlier than 10.15 to any release later than 10.15 causes the VXLAN interface configuration to be lost if the VXLAN interface is configured in a device with the Spine profile.

messages.content: For 9300 AOS-CX 10.17.1010, what limitation is mentioned for VXLAN?

Feature Caveat: VXLAN - Upgrading 9300/9300S devices from a build earlier than 10.15 to any release later than 10.15 causes the VXLAN interface configuration to be lost if the VXLAN interface is configured in a device with the Spine profile.

description: VXLAN configuration is not supported on the 9300/9300S platform when using spine or DC-Optimized-Spine profiles.

messages.content: For 9300 AOS-CX 10.17.1010, what additional caveat is documented for VXLAN?
Feature Caveat: VXLAN - VXLAN configuration is not supported on the 9300/9300S platform when using spine or DC-Optimized-Spine profiles.

description: A GRE tunnel in an access-VLAN underlay is not supported in a non-default VRF.

messages.content: For 9300 AOS-CX 10.17.1010, what caveat is documented for GRE?
Feature Caveat: GRE - A GRE tunnel in an access-VLAN underlay is not supported in a non-default VRF.

description: Overlay/system partition information is included in HOST-RESOURCES-MIB polling. The overlay partition is a read-only partition and this is reflected in SNMP queries. hrFSAccess.3 = readOnly(2) is expected behavior for overlay partitions. Monitoring tools should not treat this as an error.

messages.content: For 9300 AOS-CX 10.17.1010, what limitation is mentioned for SNMP?

Feature Caveat: SNMP - Overlay/system partition information is included in HOST-RESOURCES-MIB polling. The overlay partition is a read-only partition and this is reflected in SNMP queries. hrFSAccess.3 = readOnly(2) is expected behavior for overlay partitions. Monitoring tools should not treat this as an error.

description: Symptom: ACL logging displays incorrect ACE sequence number and action while entries are being updated in hardware. Scenario: This issue occurs when an ACL entry is configured with the logging action the logged output may report an incorrect sequence number and action. Workaround: Wait for ACL programming to finish before utilizing logs for debug/analysis of traffic.
symptom: ACL logging displays incorrect ACE sequence number and action while entries are being updated in hardware.
scenario: This issue occurs when an ACL entry is configured with the logging action the logged output may report an incorrect sequence number and action.
workaround: Wait for ACL programming to finish before utilizing logs for debug/analysis of traffic.

messages.content: For 9300 AOS-CX 10.17.1010, what issue was resolved in ACL Bug 401168?

ACL (Bug ID 401168): Symptom: ACL logging displays incorrect ACE sequence number and action while entries are being updated in hardware. Scenario: This issue occurs when an ACL entry is configured with the logging action the logged output may report an incorrect sequence number and action. Workaround: Wait for ACL programming to finish before utilizing logs for debug/analysis of traffic.

ACL (Bug ID 401168): This bug belongs to the ACL category.

description: Symptom: MACs are only showing on one side of a VSX pair. For instance, a MAC is learned on a physical port on one VSX device and is missing from the peer VSX device. Scenario: This issue can occur after resolving a network loop which causes the MACs to quickly move from one VSX device to the other, particularly when MACs are moving from EVPN tunnels to physical ports. Workaround: Using the CLI to clear the problematic MAC address.
symptom: MACs are only showing on one side of a VSX pair. For instance, a MAC is learned on a physical port on one VSX device and is missing from the peer VSX device.
scenario: This issue can occur after resolving a network loop which causes the MACs to quickly move from one VSX device to the other, particularly when MACs are moving from EVPN tunnels to physical ports.
workaround: Using the CLI to clear the problematic MAC address.

description: Symptom: hpe-restd crashes when attempting to obtain Central location from Activate. Scenario: This issue may occur if, the TLS handshake when connecting to Activate fails and causes a crash on the hpe-restd daemon (where Central connectivity resides). Workaround: DIsable the feature where Central is not being used and the crash is occurring.
symptom: hpe-restd crashes when attempting to obtain Central location from Activate.
scenario: This issue may occur if, the TLS handshake when connecting to Activate fails and causes a crash on the hpe-restd daemon (where Central connectivity resides).
workaround: DIsable the feature where Central is not being used and the crash is occurring.

messages.content: For 9300 AOS-CX 10.17.1010, what issue was resolved in Central Connectivity Bug 412351?

Central Connectivity (Bug ID 412351): Symptom: hpe-restd crashes when attempting to obtain Central location from Activate. Scenario: This issue may occur if, the TLS handshake when connecting to Activate fails and causes a crash on the hpe-restd daemon (where Central connectivity resides). Workaround: DIsable the feature where Central is not being used and the crash is occurring.

description: Symptom: CLI help string for the http protocol in the ip/ipv6 source interface command does not include https. Scenario: This issue occurs when displaying the help string for ip source-interface.
symptom: CLI help string for the http protocol in the ip/ipv6 source interface command does not include https.
scenario: This issue occurs when displaying the help string for ip source-interface.

messages.content: For 9300 AOS-CX 10.17.1010, what issue was resolved in REST Bug 411735?

REST (Bug ID 411735): Symptom: CLI help string for the http protocol in the ip/ipv6 source interface command does not include https. Scenario: This issue occurs when displaying the help string for ip source-interface.

description: Symptom: OSPF API GET requests timeout when querying OSPF neighbors via REST API, displaying "maximum entries limit" error message. Scenario: This issue may occur when previous failed REST or CLI OSPF calls leave request ID uncleaned, causing subsequent API calls to hang.
symptom: OSPF API GET requests timeout when querying OSPF neighbors via REST API, displaying "maximum entries limit" error message.
scenario: This issue may occur when previous failed REST or CLI OSPF calls leave request ID uncleaned, causing subsequent API calls to hang.

description: Symptom: High CPU utilization from PTP daemon despite PTP being disabled, causing VSF reboots with Critical Service Fault. Scenario: This issue may occur after a system boot/restart, and the interface state changes on VSF stack members, leading to an agent crash/restart during the operation.
symptom: High CPU utilization from PTP daemon despite PTP being disabled, causing VSF reboots with Critical Service Fault.

description: Symptom: Default route missing from kernel routing table, causing loss of external network connectivity, while the route remains visible in CLI output. symptom: Default route missing from kernel routing table, causing loss of external network connectivity, while the route remains visible in CLI output.
scenario: This issue may occur when interface VLAN1 is configured with DHCP, then static IP configured with same address as DHCP-assigned IP.

description: Symptom: IP SLA track objects showing Down status even when tracked IP addresses are reachable and IP SLA sessions show UP status. Scenario: This issue may occur when there are significant timing differences between IPSLA probe intervals and track object delay timer values (for example a 5-second probe interval with 30-second delay timers).
symptom: IP SLA track objects showing Down status even when tracked IP addresses are reachable and IP SLA sessions show UP status.
scenario: This issue may occur when there are significant timing differences between IPSLA probe intervals and track object delay timer values (for example a 5-second probe interval with 30-second delay timers).

description: Symptom: Classifier Policy is not enforced for traffic coming from an ISL link. Scenario: For a topology with VSX+ VRRP, if the policy is applied on SVI, all packets which reach the VRRP secondary device go to the primary device via the ISL. All of those packets are not getting classifier policy enforced. Even though the policy action is PBR redirect, those packets are not getting redirected. Workaround: Two workarounds are possible to prevent packets from being forwarded to VSX primary via ISL. 1. Use active gateway to give better active active redundancy. Packets reaching either VSX device process the packet destined to active gateway. 2. Turn off the interface connection from VRRP secondary device to down switch. This method may cause redundancy and is not preferred.

This method may cause redundancy and is not preferred.

workaround: s are possible to prevent packets from being forwarded to VSX primary via ISL. 1. Use active gateway to give better active active redundancy. Packets reaching either VSX device process the packet destined to active gateway. 2. Turn off the interface connection from VRRP secondary device to down switch. This method may cause redundancy and is not preferred.

PBR (Bug ID 407418): For a topology with VSX+ VRRP, if the policy is applied on SVI, all packets which reach the VRRP secondary device go to the primary device via the ISL. All of those packets are not getting classifier policy enforced. Even though the policy action is PBR redirect, those packets are not getting redirected.

description: Symptom: Classifier Policy is not enforced for traffic coming from an ISL link. Scenario: For a topology with VSX+ VRRP, if the policy is applied on SVI, all packets which reach the VRRP secondary device go to the primary device via the ISL. All of those packets are not getting classifier policy enforced. Even though the policy action is PBR redirect, those packets are not getting redirected. Workaround: Two workarounds are possible to prevent packets from being forwarded to VSX primary via ISL. 1. Use active gateway to give better active active redundancy. Packets reaching either VSX device process the packet destined to active gateway. 2. Turn off the interface connection from VRRP secondary device to down switch. This method may cause redundancy and is not preferred.

This method may cause redundancy and is not preferred.

description: Symptom: Traffic loss is seen when VRRP is configured on sub-interface. Scenario: This issue may occur when VRRP is configured on sub-interfaces, traffic flow through the VRRP interfaces could get impacted. Workaround: It is recommended not to have VRRP configured on sub-interfaces.

description: Symptom: Interface VXLAN configuration is disabled on 9300 and 9300S platforms for spine & DC-Optimized-Spine profiles from 10.15 onwards. Scenario: This issue may occur if using Spine profile with VXLAN configurations before 10.15 release. Workaround: Change to Leaf profile and perform switch reboot before upgrading to 10.15 or higher AOS-CX versions.
